title: Impetigo follow-up
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports documenting rapid or significant worsening, failure to improve after treatment, alternative-diagnosis concern, serious illness or cellulitis concern, and prior antibiotic exposure.
assessment.rationale: The exact reassessment section supports an impetigo follow-up record of interval change, current symptoms, distribution, systemic context, serious-illness concern and previous treatment exposure without confirming infection or changing therapy.

source.title: Impetigo: antimicrobial prescribing — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE antimicrobial and referral adaptation
source.population: Adults, children, and young people with impetigo, including reassessment after treatment or recurrent disease.
source.setting: Community, primary care, and hospital reassessment of impetigo.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting worsening, non-improvement, serious-illness features, previous antimicrobial exposure, microbiology already obtained, result review, and clinician-led referral context. Antibiotic names, doses, durations, and treatment choices are excluded.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting rapid or significant worsening, failure to improve after treatment, alternative-diagnosis concern, serious illness or cellulitis concern, and prior antibiotic exposure.

assessment.text: Supports recording skin or nasal swab context, frequent recurrence, and microbiology results already reviewed; it does not generate a test or antimicrobial change.
assessment.rationale: The microbiology section supports recording already available swab or microbiology results; it does not order a swab or alter an antimicrobial.

source.title: Impetigo: antimicrobial prescribing — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE antimicrobial and referral adaptation
source.population: Adults, children, and young people with impetigo, including reassessment after treatment or recurrent disease.
source.setting: Community, primary care, and hospital reassessment of impetigo.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting worsening, non-improvement, serious-illness features, previous antimicrobial exposure, microbiology already obtained, result review, and clinician-led referral context. Antibiotic names, doses, durations, and treatment choices are excluded.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording skin or nasal swab context, frequent recurrence, and microbiology results already reviewed; it does not generate a test or antimicrobial change.

assessment.text: Supports documenting serious illness, widespread disease with immunocompromise, bullous disease in infancy, recurrence, systemic illness, complication risk, and an already made referral or specialist discussion.
assessment.rationale: The referral section supports documenting safety-netting and an arranged follow-up or completed specialist discussion when the clinician has made it; no referral is generated.
